description: Bundle eval (DESIGN.md Q11b): empty-cwd baseline vs `skillpack scaffold react remotion`. Measures total tokens + wall-clock for an end-to-end task that exercises both project setup and the Remotion skill. This is skillpack's headline number.

evals.prompt: Build me a 15-second product-feature reel for an imaginary developer tool called 'Skiff'. It should have: (a) a title card 'Skiff' that fades in over the first 1.5 seconds and holds for 3 seconds before fading out, (b) three feature captions appearing in sequence at 5s, 8s, and 11s — each visible for ~3 seconds, with a spring-eased pop-in; the captions should read 'Type-safe by default', 'Sub-second deploys', and 'One-command setup'; (c) a subtle gradient background. The whole thing should be 1080p at 30fps. I should be able to preview it in the browser at localhost:5173 AND render it headlessly to out/video.mp4 with one command. After you finish, run the headless render and confirm out/video.mp4 exists and is non-trivial in size.
evals.expected_output: A working React project where (1) `npm run dev` boots and shows the in-browser Remotion player with the composition, (2) `npm run video:render` produces a non-zero MP4 at out/video.mp4, (3) the composition matches the title + captions + timing described, (4) the project typechecks and the existing App.test.tsx passes.

assertions.text: Project has package.json with a `video:render` script
`npm run video:render` produces out/video.mp4 with size > 50KB
Title 'Skiff' appears in the composition source
All three feature captions appear verbatim in source
AGENTS.md exists with the skillpack manifest block
